question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/AccountBalancesDownloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: shouldDownload()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the `accountBalanceFileExists` AtomicBoolean to be permanently set to `true` by triggering a single successful `onVerified()` call, so that all subsequent balance file downloads are silently skipped when `downloaderProperties.isEnabled()` is false, resulting in missing account balance records exported to mirror nodes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/AccountBalancesDownloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: shouldDownload()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `accountBalanceFileExists` is never reset to `false` after being set to `true` in `onVerified()`, causing the downloader to permanently stop fetching new balance files after the first verified file, leading to stale or missing balance records on the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/AccountBalancesDownloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: shouldDownload()] Can an unprivileged external user cause `accountBalanceFileRepository.findLatest()` to return a present value (e.g., by injecting a synthetic balance file record into the database through an exposed API) so that `accountBalanceFileExists` is set to `true` and `shouldDownload()` permanently returns `false`, causing all future balance downloads to be skipped?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/AccountBalancesDownloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: onVerified()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a malformed `AccountBalanceFile` that passes signature verification but contains incorrect balance data, so that `onVerified()` calls `super.onVerified()` and sets `accountBalanceFileExists` to `true`, causing the incorrect balance records to be exported to the mirror node while future correct files are skipped?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/AccountBalancesDownloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: shouldDownload()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a race condition between the `accountBalanceFileExists.get()` check and the `accountBalanceFileRepository.findLatest()` call in `shouldDownload()`, where two concurrent scheduler threads both see `accountBalanceFileExists` as `false` and both proceed to download, causing duplicate or out-of-order balance records exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/AccountBalancesDownloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: shouldDownload()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the non-atomic check-then-act pattern in `shouldDownload()` — where `accountBalanceFileExists.get()` is checked and then `accountBalanceFileRepository.findLatest()` is called separately — to cause a TOCTOU race that results in the downloader skipping a balance file that should have been downloaded, leading to a gap in exported records?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/AccountBalancesDownloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: download()] Can an unprivileged external user cause `downloadNextBatch()` to silently swallow a `SignatureVerificationException` (caught in the parent `Downloader`) so that a balance file with a valid but attacker-influenced hash is skipped without any retry, resulting in a missing balance record on the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/AccountBalancesDownloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: shouldDownload()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `BalanceDownloaderProperties.enabled` defaults to `false`, combined with the one-shot download logic in `shouldDownload()`, to ensure only a single balance file is ever downloaded after node startup, causing all subsequent balance snapshots to be missing from the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/AccountBalancesDownloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: onVerified()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a balance file with a manipulated `consensusTimestamp` that is accepted by `onVerified()` and stored via `streamFileNotifier.verified()`, causing the `lastStreamFile` reference in the parent `Downloader` to advance past legitimate files, so those files are never re-downloaded and their records are missing from the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/AccountBalancesDownloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: shouldDownload()] Can an unprivileged external user cause `accountBalanceFileRepository.findLatest()` to return an `Optional` with a stale or incorrect `AccountBalanceFile` (e.g., one with a future `consensus_timestamp`), so that `shouldDownload()` sets `accountBalanceFileExists` to `true` and stops all further downloads, causing missing balance records on the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/AccountBalancesDownloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: download()] Can an unprivileged external user flood the cloud storage bucket with a large number of malformed balance signature files, causing `downloadAndParseSigFiles()` to spend its entire timeout processing invalid signatures and returning an empty `sigFilesMap`, so that no valid balance files are downloaded and records are missing from the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/AccountBalancesDownloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: shouldDownload()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `shouldDownload()` returns `true` only once (when `enabled=false` and no file exists) by causing the single allowed download attempt to fail silently, so that `accountBalanceFileExists` remains `false` but the downloader never retries, resulting in permanently missing balance records?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/AccountBalancesDownloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: onVerified()] Can an unprivileged external user cause `super.onVerified()` to update `lastStreamFile` with an `AccountBalanceFile` whose `getName()` returns a lexicographically later filename than the actual next file, so that `getStartAfterFilename()` skips legitimate balance files and they are never downloaded, causing missing records on the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/AccountBalancesDownloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: download()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `onErrorContinue` in `downloadAndParseSigFiles()` to suppress errors from all nodes simultaneously, causing `sigFilesMap` to be empty and no balance files to be downloaded, resulting in missing balance records exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/AccountBalancesDownloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: shouldDownload()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a database connection failure during `accountBalanceFileRepository.findLatest()` that propagates as an unchecked exception through `shouldDownload()`, crashing the scheduled `download()` task and causing the Spring scheduler to stop rescheduling it, resulting in all future balance files being missing from the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/AccountBalancesDownloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: onVerified()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a balance file where `streamFile.getConsensusEnd()` returns a value that causes `cloudStorageLatencyMetric.record()` to record a negative duration, corrupting monitoring metrics so that operators are unaware that balance records are missing from the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/AccountBalancesDownloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: download()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `listLimit = 1` behavior (when `downloaderProperties.isEnabled()` is false) in `downloadAndParseSigFiles()` to ensure only one signature file is ever fetched per download cycle, causing the downloader to process only one balance file and miss all subsequent ones, resulting in incomplete records on the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/AccountBalancesDownloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: shouldDownload()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the ordering of checks in `shouldDownload()` — first `downloaderProperties.isEnabled()`, then `accountBalanceFileExists.get()`, then `accountBalanceFileRepository.findLatest()` — to cause a scenario where the downloader permanently returns `false` after a transient DB error sets `accountBalanceFileExists` incorrectly, leading to missing balance records?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/AccountBalancesDownloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: onVerified()] Can an unprivileged external user cause `streamFileNotifier.verified(streamFile)` to throw a runtime exception after `accountBalanceFileExists.set(true)` has already been called in `onVerified()`, so that the balance file is marked as existing but not actually notified/persisted, causing the mirror node to export incorrect (missing) balance records while the downloader believes it has already processed the file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/AccountBalancesDownloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: shouldDownload()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `accountBalanceFileExists` is an instance-level `AtomicBoolean` (not persisted) so that after a JVM restart or bean re-initialization, the flag resets to `false` and the downloader re-downloads the same balance file, causing duplicate records to be exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/AccountBalancesDownloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: download()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the `@Scheduled` task to be invoked concurrently (e.g., by exploiting a misconfigured task executor) so that two `downloadNextBatch()` calls run simultaneously, both passing `shouldDownload()` checks and both attempting to process the same balance file, resulting in duplicate or corrupted records exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/AccountBalancesDownloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: shouldDownload()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `shouldDownload()` calls `accountBalanceFileRepository.findLatest()` on every invocation (when `accountBalanceFileExists` is false) to cause a DB query amplification attack that degrades performance, indirectly causing the scheduler to miss download windows and resulting in missing balance records on the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/AccountBalancesDownloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: onVerified()] Can an unprivileged external user cause `super.onVerified()` to update `lastStreamFile` with a copy of the `AccountBalanceFile` that has `bytes` and `items` set to `null` (via `streamFile.copy().clear()`), and then exploit a downstream consumer that expects non-null bytes, causing it to export incorrect or empty balance records to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/AccountBalancesDownloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: download()] Can an unprivileged external user place a balance file with a timestamp that is lexicographically before `StreamFilename.EPOCH` in the cloud storage bucket, causing `getStartAfterFilename()` to return `EPOCH` and the file to be perpetually re-listed but never processed, resulting in the downloader being stuck and missing all newer balance records?
